feed.title: DONGHO KANG
feed.subtitle: I am a doctoral student at ETH Zurich and a research assistant at Computational Robotics Lab. My research aims to create a legged robot that can perform natural, animal-like motion. Thus, my research interests are broad ranging to legged locomotion control, computational model of character animation and design optimization for robotics applications.

entry.title: Building Bio-inspired Quadrupedal Robots
entry.content:
We are building bio-inspired quadrupedal robots. Our goal is to design mechanisms and control strategies that can generate more animal-like walking motion for legged robots and eventually to bring those robots to people's daily life.

Our research interests are broad-ranging from control and planning of legged robots to design optimization, digital fabrication and finally human-robot interaction.

Student Projects

We are currently looking for self-motivated Master students who wants to participate in one of our 3-month (14-weeks, part-time) or 6-month (full-time) research projects. Please send your CV and short motivation letter to Dongho Kang if you are interested in.

Available Topics

Real robot deployment of model-based controller

  • Semester project (14 weeks)
  • Starting date: From July, 2021
  • Prerequisite courses:
    • Robot Dynamics or Computational Models of Motion for Character Animation and Robotics (required)
    • Recursive Estimation (required)
    • Model Predictive Control (optional)
  • Required skills:
    • C++ programming (required)
    • experiences with ROS (optional)

In this project, you will implement one of the current state-of-the-art model-based controller or state estimation algorithm for legged robots in C++. We aim to deploy the new controller/estimator to Unitree A1 quadrupedal robot, and benchmark the performance.

Note. this project is a rather research engineering project requires rich engineering experiences in control and robot programming. We do not aim to publish a paper.

An automatic control gain tuning algorithm for legged robot locomotion controllers

  • Master's thesis (6 months, full-time)
  • Starting date: TBD
  • Prerequisite courses:
    • Robot Dynamics or Computational Models of Motion for Character Animation and Robotics
  • Required skills:
    • C++ programming (required)
    • experiences with ROS (optional)

Kinematic mapping for motion generation of quadrupedal locomotion

  • Master's thesis (6 months, full-time)
  • Starting date: TBD
  • Prerequisite courses:
    • Robot Dynamics or Computational Models of Motion for Character Animation and Robotics
    • Deep Learning
  • Required skills:
    • C++ and Python programming (required)
    • Deep Learning

For students from research or educational institutes other than ETH

The Master's semester project and thesis project are part of the Master's degree program at ETH Zurich and we cannot provide any financial support for cost of living and neither legal support for your visa process. However, there are plenty of opportunities to visit ETH for research exchanging purposes. So please make sure you can visit ETH and Switzerland with legitimated permission supported by one of these programmes before you contact us.

  • Exchange and visiting studies
  • ETH Student Summer Research Fellowship (Department of Computer Science)
  • ETH Robotics Student Fellowship
entry.summary: We are building bio-inspired quadrupedal robots. Our goal is to design mechanisms and control strategies that can generate more animal-like walking motion for legged robots and eventually to bring those robots to people's daily life.

entry.title: UnityMeshImporter: a run-time mesh importer for Unity

link.title: UnityMeshImporter: a run-time mesh importer for Unity

entry.content:
Unity does not support run-time mesh import from files outside of the project for performance. Fortunately, AssimpNet provides nice Unity plugin enables loading various formats of mesh objects.

I wrote a Unity package based on this: UnityMeshImporter. This package loads mesh file by AssimpNet and convert it to a Unity GameObject. The source code is available on GitHub.

It just works like this:


Very simple!

You can find a simple example Unity app here. Please star the projects if you like it. Any comments or issue rerporting through GitHub issue is welcomed. This project is also used for RaiSimUity project.
entry.summary: Unity does not support run-time mesh import from files outside of the project for performance. Fortunately, AssimpNet provides nice Unity plugin enables loading various formats of mesh objects.

entry.title: SimBenchmark: Physics Engine Benchmark for Robotics Applications

link.title: SimBenchmark: Physics Engine Benchmark for Robotics Applications

entry.content:
SimBenchmark is a benchmark suite for state-of-the-art physics engines.

I worked on this project while I was developing RAISIM. We evaluated a few widely used physics engines for robotics and machine learning applications. The list of the engines is as follows:

  • RAISIM
  • Bullet Physics
  • Open Dynamics Engine
  • Multi-Joint dynamics with Contact (a.k.a. MuJoCo)
  • DART Sim

More details are on the SimBenchmark website
entry.summary: SimBenchmark is a benchmark suite for state-of-the-art physics engines.

entry.title: Exercises of Programming for Robotics – ROS

link.title: Exercises of Programming for Robotics – ROS

entry.content:
Programming for Robotics - ROS course is an open lecture by Prof. Marco Hutter of ETH Zürich Robotic System Laboratory. The course materials and online video lectures are avilable on the website.

I opened Github repository contains resources and source code for exercises from myself with Prof. Hutter's permission. Please use it for only private purposes.

Programming for Robotics - ROS는 ETH Zürich Robotic Systems Lab의 Marco Hutter 교수님이 2016년 2월에 개설한 오픈강좌입니다. 수업자료와 비디오 강의가 공개되어 있습니다.

일전에 개인적으로 문의하신 분들이 있어서 교수님의 허락을 받아 수업시간에 제가 작성한 실습코드를 공개합니다. 참고용으로 활용하실 수 있습니다.

소스코드는 Github repository 에서 내려받을 수 있습니다. 해당 실습이 ETH Zürich 학생들의 성적평가에 반영되기 때문에 무단으로 배포하지 말아주시길 부탁드립니다.
entry.summary: Programming for Robotics - ROS course is an open lecture by Prof. Marco Hutter of ETH Zürich Robotic System Laboratory. The course materials and online video lectures are avilable on the website.
